content:
I am trying out b4 trailers command for the first time. Is this the expected behaviour? Here is the link to the patch

image.png
From this output I think it might only add the trailers to the first commit.

Let me know if I need to add trailers manually to each commit.
If b4 doesn't do the right thing, you can always change the commits manually, yes
Quick question. Am I supposed to use these trailers in the second commit in v6 as well?

I'm not using b4 to send patches, so I don't exactly know how to read this, but I can tell you what the result should look like:

Patches 1 & 2 should have my ACK, all three patches should have Alice's RB.
Thanks for comment. I will add these and submit v6.

I often experience that b4 trailers does not do what I want. Some times b4 will apply a tag to all emails in a series even if it was only given on a specific patch. I never dug into it though. I hear that Konstantin, the maintainer of b4, is quite helpful in fixing issues if you reach out.

Please note that b4 is not meant to fully automate the tag workflow. It is helpful to do most of the tedious work, but one still needs to decide for each tag if it is meant to be picked up or not and so on.

For instance, sometimes tags are conditional, sometimes need to be dropped (because the new version may be significantly different), and so on and so forth.
shivamklr has marked this topic as resolved.
